title: Required Capabilities Manifest
description: Declares the OS-level capabilities a package requires. This file is OPTIONAL — packages without it are treated as pure computation with zero OS access (default deny). Only packages that need OS capabilities should have this file. Any non-empty capability requires hardware-key-signed approval.

$schema.description: Reference to this JSON Schema for editor autocompletion.

version.description: Schema version. Currently 1. Allows schema evolution without breaking existing manifests.

package.description: Qualified package name matching the build tool's naming: {language}/{dirname}. Examples: python/logic-gates, ruby/lexer, go/cache, rust/json-value, elixir/json_value, lua/grammar_tools.

capabilities.description: List of OS-level capabilities this package requires. An empty array means the package is pure computation with zero OS interaction — this is the expected state for most packages.

justification.description: Human-readable explanation of the overall capability profile. Should explain WHY the package needs (or does not need) OS access.

banned_construct_exceptions.description: Exceptions to the banned dynamic execution constructs (eval, exec, __import__, import "C", plugin.Open, etc.). Each exception requires hardware-key-signed approval. Some languages also require a matching capability declaration, such as Go ffi:call/load for native interop. Most packages should not have any exceptions.

category.description: The kind of OS resource being accessed.

action.description: The operation being performed on the resource.

target.description: The specific resource being accessed. Format depends on category: fs uses relative/absolute paths with globs (e.g., '../../grammars/*.tokens'), net uses host:port (e.g., 'api.example.com:443'), proc uses command names (e.g., 'git'), env uses variable names (e.g., 'HOME'), ffi uses library names (e.g., 'libssl'). Use '*' for broad access (discouraged).

justification.description: Human-readable explanation of WHY this specific capability is needed. Should be specific enough that a reviewer can evaluate whether the access is legitimate.

construct.description: The banned construct being exempted (e.g., 'eval', 'compile', '__import__', 'reflect.Value.Call', 'import "C"', or 'plugin.Open').

language.description: The language this exception applies to.

justification.description: Why this package needs this banned construct. Must explain what it's used for and why alternatives are insufficient.
